scoring_methodology: Strict: BEAT = actual above HIGH end of guidance (1.0); HIT = within range (0.5); MISS = below LOW end (0.0). Reported MCS = weighted average.
name: Netflix, Inc.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Next earnings: 2026-07-16 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: Sector thesis (Communication Services): Ad spending normalization + AI-driven engagement reshaping the long-run growth profile.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 37.4% — normal.
Put/Call OI: 0.64 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).
30-day implied move: ±7.9% ($78.74 – $92.16) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: +3.12pp — put skew (downside hedges bid).
Front-month OI cluster: $1.5 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Calendar spread at $85 strike: short 30 DTE / long 60 DTE. Mid IV, range-bound → harvest near-term theta while keeping back-month optionality.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 36.6% across 11 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 11 dates spanning 101–948 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±18.6% ($69.52 – $101.38).
220-day implied move: ±28.1% ($61.42 – $109.48) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Flat term structure (-0.9pp) — no near-term event premium; long-dated vol fairly priced.
long_term.strategy: Long 1-year ATM straddle at $85. Low IV (37%) + range-bound → buy volatility cheap ahead of a 12-month catalyst window; profits if move >±37%.
